session.tool_result: docs/learned/planning/tripwires.md:253:**using only branch-based discovery for plan/objective resolution** → Read [Objective Update After Land](objective-update-after-land.md) first. Use direct lookup with --plan parameter when available. Branch-based discovery fails if the branch is already deleted. Direct lookup with fallback is the resilient pattern.

session.tool_result:
80→| Contradiction Type | Example | Section to Update | 81→| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------- | 82→| **Decision override** | Objective says "Use polling", PR implemented WebSockets | Design Decisions | 83→| **Scope change** | Node says "Add 3 methods", PR only needed 2 | Node description in roadmap | 84→| **Architecture drift** | Context says "config in config.py", PR moved it to settings/ | Implementation Context | 85→| **Constraint invalidation** | Requirement listed is no longer valid | Implementation Context | 86→| **New discovery** | PR revealed a caching bug affecting future nodes | Implementation Context or new Design Decision | 87→ 88→**If prose reconciliation found stale sections**, update the objective's first comment. Parse `objective_comment_id` from `objective.body`'s `objective-header` metadata block, then update: 89→ 90→```bash 91→gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/comments/{comment_id} -X PATCH -f body="<updated comment body>" 92→``` 93→ 94→### Step 3: Closing Triggers 95→ 96→**If `auto_closed: true` in Step 1 output:** The exec command already closed the objective and posted the "Objective Complete" comment. Report: "All nodes complete - objective closed automatically" and show the objective URL. Skip the rest of Step 3. 97→ 98→Use `roadmap.all_complete` from Step 1 output as the primary signal. Additionally, if `node_updates` from Step 1 was empty (auto-match found no nodes) AND Step 2 prose reconciliation confirmed all roadmap nodes are now done, treat `all_complete` as `true` for the purposes of this step. 99→ 100→**If `all_complete` is `true`:** 101→ 102→- Ask the user directly: 103→ ``` 104→ All roadmap nodes are complete. Should I close objective #<number> now? 105→ - Yes, close with final summary 106→ - Not yet, there may be follow-up work 107→ - I'll close it manually later 108→ ``` 109→ If yes: post final comment and close. Otherwise: acknowledge and report complete.
... [5 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Prompt looks correct. Let me also verify the Step 2 additions:
session.tool_result:
63→Compare `objective.objective_content` (the prose from the first comment's `objective-body` block) against what the PR actually did. 64→ 65→If `objective_content` is null, skip prose reconciliation entirely (objective has no prose comment). 66→ 67→- Read each Design Decision. Did the PR override or refine any? 68→- Read Implementation Context. Does the architecture description still match? 69→- Read upcoming node descriptions. Did this PR change the landscape? 70→- If nothing is stale, skip prose update entirely. 71→ 72→**If `node_updates` from Step 1 was empty** (auto-match found no nodes) and the roadmap has non-terminal nodes (`in_progress` or `pending`), assess during prose reconciliation whether any of those nodes were completed by this PR. For each node the PR completed: 73→ 74→```bash 75→erk exec update-objective-node <objective_number> --node <node_id> --status done --pr "#<pr_number>" 76→``` 77→ 78→This keeps the YAML accurate and ensures `all_complete` reflects the true state for Step 3. 79→ 80→| Contradiction Type | Example | Section to Update | 81→| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------- | 82→| **Decision override** | Objective says "Use polling", PR implemented WebSockets | Design Decisions |
